title: Cordyline roxburghiana
taxonomy: Cordyline roxburghiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. This plant is native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. It is a member of the genus Cordyline, which includes several species of palm-like plants. Cordyline roxburghiana is further classified within the tribe Cordylineae and the subfamily Hemerocallidoideae. The plant's classification is also recognized in botanical systems, such as the APG III system.
uses: Cordyline roxburghiana has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments such as fever and inflammation. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and constipation. Additionally, it has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

intro: Cordyline roxburghiana, commonly known as New Zealand cabbage tree or harakeke, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its tall, slender stems and fan-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues.
